title: Generated CLI dogfooding follow-ups

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Fix dogfooding issues #996 and #997 before continuing the #892 Python generated CLI epic.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep this lane limited to generated CLI dogfooding follow-ups: serial proof safety, compatibility naming, static proof, and planning closeout evidence.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact proof metadata shape, compatibility wrappers, source/test renames, static allowlists, and focused validation commands.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A fix requires changing #892 architecture direction, removing required compatibility behavior, or broadening into TypeScript/Bash/PowerShell implementation.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement #996 first, commit and push, then implement #997.
completion_criteria: #996 serial proof safety is implemented, validated, committed, pushed, and issue state updated.
#997 compatibility naming/proof cleanup is implemented, validated, committed, pushed, and issue state updated.
The active plan records proof, cost assessment, durable residue routing, and honest continuation into #892.

goal: Fix dogfooding issues #996 and #997 before continuing the #892 Python generated CLI epic.
non_goals: Do not resume broader #892 architecture work until #996 and #997 have been addressed.
Do not expand into TypeScript, Bash, PowerShell, or MCP implementation.

intent.outcome: Fix dogfooding issues #996 and #997 before continuing the #892 Python generated CLI epic.
intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to generated CLI dogfooding follow-ups: serial proof safety, compatibility naming, static proof, and planning closeout evidence.
intent.latitude: Exact proof metadata shape, compatibility wrappers, source/test renames, static allowlists, and focused validation commands.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes #892 architecture direction, removes required compatibility behavior, or broadens into non-Python targets.

execution.next_step: Implement #996 first, commit and push, then implement #997.
execution.proof: Generated command package proof, focused generated-package tests, contract tooling tests, proof-routing tests, and ruff.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Keep the #892 Python generated CLI epic honest by fixing dogfooding gaps before further implementation.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Patch serial proof safety first, then compatibility naming/proof cleanup.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Generated CLI proof contracts, generated-package checker, command-generation loader/console helpers, generated CLI compatibility aliases when regenerated, focused tests, and this execplan.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Approximately 12 files per issue slice; stop and reassess if the change wants a repo-wide rename beyond explicit compatibility surfaces.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Generated package proof, focused pytest for generated-package proof/tooling/proof routing, ruff for touched Python, and generator freshness when generated output changes.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing public command semantics, removing installed bridge compatibility, or widening into non-Python targets.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, TypeScript/Bash/PowerShell/MCP implementation, or #892 architecture beyond the dogfooding follow-up issues.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Current lane: #996 serial proof safety, then #997 compatibility naming/proof cleanup; commit and push after each slice.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
